prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing; ongoing transmission in some states and districts

riskFactors: limited access to trained examiners in rural areas
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical signs (hypopigmented/erythematous anesthetic lesions, nerve thickening) with smear/biopsy support; classification per WHO/NLEP

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: contact screening and targeted case detection campaigns in endemic blocks
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High cure rates with MDT; disability prevention limited by late presentation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: NLEP supports decentralized diagnosis in primary care with referral for reactions/neuritis
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: WHO MDT provided free via NLEP; corticosteroids for reactions and neuritis; disability prevention and rehabilitation services where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and delayed diagnosis, disability burden, and uneven access to rehabilitation and specialist care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Long-standing NLEP infrastructure with free MDT and community-based detection activities in endemic areas
